basics.name: Kilian Pouderoux
basics.label: Student
basics.summary: Final year Master’s student in Microengineering at EPFL and CentraleSupélec. With expertise in microsystems, nanotechnology, and computational science, I bring strong curiosity, organization, and autonomy to my work. I am eager to contribute to high-tech, fast-moving projects and am seeking a 6-month internship starting in February 2026.

education.institution: Ecole Polytechnique Fédérale de Lausanne (EPFL), Lausanne, Switzerland
education.area: Microengineering, minor in Computational Science and Engineering
Engineering, highly selective double degree with EPFL

education.institution: Ecole Polytechnique Fédérale de Lausanne (EPFL), Lausanne, Switzerland

work.summary: Interdisciplinary research internship in Leuven, Belgium, combining microfluidics, photonics, and systems engineering to develop an integrated on-chip cell sorting platform for biomedical diagnostics applications.
highlights: Design and assemble an integrated optical and microfluidic setup, combining fluidic, electronic, and photonic components
Develop data acquisition and processing pipelines to analyze and visualize cell sorting performance metrics
Prepare biological samples and conduct wet lab experiments to characterize the chip's sorting performance

work.summary: Diagnosing, maintaining, and optimizing a LabVIEW-based machine in a high-precision manufacturing environment.

work.company: Safran Landing Systems (Mission Junior Entreprise CentraleSupélec)
work.summary: Analyzed technical documents for more than 60 pieces of equipment and retrieved the associated data from the PDM system.
Discovered the operating environment of a vacuum-technology company by preparing orders for the shipping and production departments and handling inventory and delivery errors.

work.company: École Polytechnique Fédérale de Lausanne, EPFL
work.summary: Teaching assistant for several undergraduate courses including Materials (chemistry to properties), Programming for Engineers, Mathematics, and Information, Computation and Communication.

volunteer.summary: Student association focused on the design, development, and launch of innovative satellite and space related projects.
highlights: Lead a team of 8 to develop and operate ground stations for satellite communications.
Develop and assemble an X-band antenna, the first student-operated system of its kind.
Design and implement the antenna’s control software.

volunteer.summary: Student association focused on the design, development, and launch of innovative satellite and space related projects.
highlights: Contribute to a digital twin for satellite modeling, enabling faster design iterations and performance optimization.
Perform tradeoff analyses on critical satellite parameters, guiding key design decisions and improving mission reliability.

volunteer.summary: Oversee the budget of the EPFL & UNIL student surf association to ensure its financial health and sustainability. Manage member registrations and coordinate event logistics across water-sport and community activities.
Organized surf, pumpfoil, winch surf, and wakesurf sessions for EPFL & UNIL students. Managed partnerships and sponsorship acquisition to support the association's activities.

Managed partner-company relations and event logistics for a major business forum with 200+ exhibitors and a €1.3M turnover, while handling quality management through the annual audit and ISO compliance. Developed automation algorithms to streamline repetitive tasks and improve team efficiency.

awards.title: B+ Scholarship
awards.summary: Scholarships awarded to incoming freshmen and continuing students who complete a rigorous high school curriculum with a minimum overall GPA of 3.0 at a Hawai'i public school.

awards.title: First place
awards.summary: My team won first place in 2015.  See my portfolio site for more details.
